# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Rails mailer class that sends emails via SendGrid. Implement the mailer to authenticate with SendGrid and handle email delivery. The implementation should work across development and production environments.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

require 'sendgrid-ruby'

class SendGridMailer < ApplicationMailer
  include SendGrid

  default from: ENV.fetch('SENDGRID_DEFAULT_FROM', 'noreply@example.com')

  def initialize
    super
    @sendgrid_api_key = ENV.fetch('SENDGRID_API_KEY', nil)
  end

  def welcome_email(user)
    @user = user
    @url = root_url

    if use_sendgrid?
      deliver_via_sendgrid(
        to: @user.email,
        subject: 'Welcome to Our Application',
        template_id: ENV.fetch('SENDGRID_WELCOME_TEMPLATE_ID', nil),
        dynamic_template_data: {
          user_name: @user.name,
          login_url: @url
        }
      )
    else
      mail(
        to: @user.email,
        subject: 'Welcome to Our Application'
      )
    end
  end

  def password_reset_email(user, reset_token)
    @user = user
    @reset_token = reset_token
    @reset_url = edit_password_reset_url(reset_token)

    if use_sendgrid?
      deliver_via_sendgrid(
        to: @user.email,
        subject: 'Password Reset Instructions',
        template_id: ENV.fetch('SENDGRID_PASSWORD_RESET_TEMPLATE_ID', nil),
        dynamic_template_data: {
          user_name: @user.name,
          reset_url: @reset_url
        }
      )
    else
      mail(
        to: @user.email,
        subject: 'Password Reset Instructions'
      )
    end
  end

  def notification_email(user, subject:, body:, category: nil)
    @user = user
    @body = body

    if use_sendgrid?
      deliver_via_sendgrid(
        to: @user.email,
        subject: subject,
        body: body,
        category: category
      )
    else
      mail(
        to: @user.email,
        subject: subject
      )
    end
  end

  def custom_email(to:, subject:, html_content: nil, plain_content: nil, from: nil, cc: nil, bcc: nil, attachments_list: [], category: nil, send_at: nil)
    if use_sendgrid?
      deliver_via_sendgrid(
        to: to,
        from: from,
        subject: subject,
        html_content: html_content,
        plain_content: plain_content,
        cc: cc,
        bcc: bcc,
        attachments_list: attachments_list,
        category: category,
        send_at: send_at
      )
    else
      mail_options = {
        to: to,
        subject: subject
      }
      mail_options[:from] = from if from
      mail_options[:cc] = cc if cc
      mail_options[:bcc] = bcc if bcc

      attachments_list.each do |attachment_data|
        attachments[attachment_data[:filename]] = {
          mime_type: attachment_data[:type],
          content: attachment_data[:content]
        }
      end

      mail(mail_options) do |format|
        format.html { render html: html_content.html_safe } if html_content
        format.text { render plain: plain_content } if plain_content
      end
    end
  end

  private

  def use_sendgrid?
    @sendgrid_api_key.present? && !Rails.env.test?
  end

  def deliver_via_sendgrid(options = {})
    mail_object = SendGrid::Mail.new

    from_email = options[:from] || self.class.default[:from]
    mail_object.from = Email.new(email: from_email)

    mail_object.subject = options[:subject]

    personalization = Personalization.new
    add_recipients(personalization, :to, options[:to])
    add_recipients(personalization, :cc, options[:cc]) if options[:cc]
    add_recipients(personalization, :bcc, options[:bcc]) if options[:bcc]

    if options[:dynamic_template_data]
      options[:dynamic_template_data].each do |key, value|
        personalization.add_dynamic_template_data(key.to_s => value)
      end
    end

    if options[:send_at]
      personalization.send_at = options[:send_at].to_i
    end

    mail_object.add_personalization(personalization)

    if options[:template_id]
      mail_object.template_id = options[:template_id]
    else
      if options[:html_content]
        mail_object.add_content(Content.new(type: 'text/html', value: options[:html_content]))
      end

      if options[:plain_content]
        mail_object.add_content(Content.new(type: 'text/plain', value: options[:plain_content]))
      elsif options[:body]
        mail_object.add_content(Content.new(type: 'text/plain', value: options[:body]))
      end

      if !options[:html_content] && !options[:plain_content] && !options[:body]
        rendered = render_to_string(formats: [:html])
        mail_object.add_content(Content.new(type: 'text/html', value: rendered))
      rescue ActionView::MissingTemplate
        Rails.logger.warn("SendGridMailer: No content or template provided for email to #{options[:to]}")
      end
    end

    if options[:category]
      mail_object.add_category(Category.new(name: options[:category]))
    end

    if options[:attachments_list]&.any?
      options[:attachments_list].each do |attachment_data|
        attachment = Attachment.new
        attachment.content = Base64.strict_encode64(attachment_data[:content])
        attachment.type = attachment_data[:type]
        attachment.filename = attachment_data[:filename]
        attachment.disposition = attachment_data[:disposition] || 'attachment'
        mail_object.add_attachment(attachment)
      end
    end

    # Enable tracking settings
    tracking = TrackingSettings.new
    click_tracking = ClickTracking.new
    click_tracking.enable = true
    click_tracking.enable_text = false
    tracking.click_tracking = click_tracking

    open_tracking = OpenTracking.new
    open_tracking.enable = true
    tracking.open_tracking = open_tracking

    mail_object.tracking_settings = tracking

    send_with_sendgrid(mail_object)
  end

  def add_recipients(personalization, type, addresses)
    return unless addresses

    Array(addresses).each do |address|
      email = Email.new(email: address)
      case type
      when :to
        personalization.add_to(email)
      when :cc
        personalization.add_cc(email)
      when :bcc
        personalization.add_bcc(email)
      end
    end
  end

  def send_with_sendgrid(mail_object)
    sg = SendGrid::API.new(api_key: @sendgrid_api_key)

    retries = 0
    max_retries = 3

    begin
      response = sg.client.mail._('send').post(request_body: mail_object.to_json)

      case response.status_code.to_i
      when 200, 201, 202
        Rails.logger.info("SendGridMailer: Email sent successfully. Status: #{response.status_code}")
        response
      when 429
        if retries < max_retries
          retries += 1
          wait_time = 2**retries
          Rails.logger.warn("SendGridMailer: Rate limited. Retrying in #{wait_time} seconds (attempt #{retries}/#{max_retries})")
          sleep(wait_time)
          retry
        else
          handle_sendgrid_error(response)
        end
      else
        handle_sendgrid_error(response)
      end
    rescue StandardError => e
      if retries < max_retries
        retries += 1
        wait_time = 2**retries
        Rails.logger.warn("SendGridMailer: Error '#{e.message}'. Retrying in #{wait_time} seconds (attempt #{retries}/#{max_retries})")
        sleep(wait_time)
        retry
      else
        Rails.logger.error("SendGridMailer: Failed after #{max_retries} retries. Error: #{e.message}")
        Rails.logger.error(e.backtrace.first(10).join("\n"))
        raise SendGridDeliveryError, "Failed to send email via SendGrid: #{e.message}"
      end
    end
  end

  def handle_sendgrid_error(response)
    error_message = "SendGrid API error - Status: #{response.status_code}, Body: #{response.body}"
    Rails.logger.error("SendGridMailer: #{error_message}")

    raise SendGridDeliveryError, error_message
  end

  class SendGridDeliveryError < StandardError; end
end
